mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Historical inverse correlation between Fed funds rate and equity multiples during easing cycles
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points within one month signals deteriorating labor market conditions, triggering risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Unemployment spikes historically precede equity drawdowns within one month
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment threshold breach, or the forward return distribution shows no statistical difference from non-triggered periods.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts exceed 1.5 million units while industrial production falls below 99.0, the divergence indicates demand shifting from goods to services, causing sector rotation away from cyclicals and negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Goods-to-services demand shift historically compresses industrial equity valuations
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict forward returns with Rank IC > 0.05 over a 3-year rolling window, or single-proxy models outperform the interaction term in out-of-sample Sharpe ratios.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of rapid monetary tightening, rising real rates combined with contracting real personal savings drain consumer spending capacity, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Real savings depletion historically precedes consumption-driven equity corrections
failure_condition: Forward returns in tightening regimes do not exhibit statistically significant negative skew compared to easing regimes, or the interaction between rates and savings shows zero predictive power in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly contraction in the industrial production index below 99.0 signals supply chain bottlenecks or demand destruction, causing immediate negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as earnings revisions turn negative.
evidence_refs: fact:Industrial production declines historically trigger immediate equity repricing
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in > 55% of instances following the index breach, or the signal's information coefficient drops below 0.02 over any 12-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sequential quarterly increase in real GDP growth exceeding 2.0 percent annually confirms robust aggregate demand, supporting corporate revenue expansion and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:GDP growth and equity earnings growth exhibit strong contemporaneous correlation
failure_condition: The hypothesis fails if 20-day forward returns are not significantly positive when GDP growth exceeds 2.0%, or if the predictive Rank IC falls below 0.04 over a 3-year out-of-sample period.

mechanism_narrative: When nonfarm payrolls grow by more than 200,000 while the federal funds rate remains below 0.5 percent, the combination signals accommodative policy supporting wage-driven consumption without triggering inflation fears, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Strong labor markets under low rates historically drive equity risk premium expansion
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to generate positive forward returns in > 60% of occurrences, or single-proxy models consistently outperform the interaction term in out-of-sample directional accuracy.

mechanism_narrative: In late-cycle expansions where housing starts peak above 1.6 million units and unemployment falls below 4.0 percent, overheating demand triggers anticipatory rate hikes and valuation compression, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Late-cycle labor and housing peaks historically precede equity corrections within one month
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are not significantly negative when both housing and labor indicators hit late-cycle thresholds, or the signal's out-of-sample directional accuracy drops below 50% over a 5-year rolling window.
